Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 4, 134 [ritual against ghost][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (missing)
Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] ŠU-MIN ⸢d15 UŠ₂⸣-MEŠ ša₂ IGI-MEŠ ana KU₅si!(.IS) A ID₂ A NIG₂.⸢AR₃.RA⸣

(r 1) [...] hand of Ištar. In order to keep away the dead who keep appearing. Water from the river, infusion of mundu-flour [...] one by one you mix (them) up with each other. You fill the horn of an ox; [...] the horn of an ox with your right hand you raise [...] with your left hand and you speak as follows:

r 22

[... a-ḫe]-⸢en⸣-na-a ana a-ḫa-meš ḪI.ḪI SI GU₄ SA₅ SI GU₄ ina 15-ka

r 33

[...] ina GUB₃-ka IL₂-ma kam DU₁₁.GA

r 44

[...] munusiš-ta-ri nap-li-si ag-ga ŠA₃-ku-nu li-nu-ḫa

(r 4) "[...] my goddess, look favourably upon (me)! May you appease your angry hearts!

r 55

[lip-pa-aš]-ra ka-bat-ta-ku-nu SILIM.MA šuk-na-ni

(r 5) May your moods be reconciled (with me)! Provide me (with) peace!"

r 66

[...] GA MA A-MEŠ ša₂ SI GU₄ GI.IZI.LA₂ u₂-kab-bit-ma UŠ₂-MEŠ KU₅

(r 6) [...] ... he extinguishes the torch (with) the water from the ox's horn and the dead will be kept away.

r 77

[...] MAŠ₂.GE₆-šu₂ UŠ₂-MEŠ IGI-⸢MEŠ GAZIsar KUR.KUR⸣ EME.UR.GI₇ tug₂NIG₂.DARA₂.ŠU.LAL₂

(r 7) [...] (in) his dream, dead who keep appearing: [...] mustard, atāʾišu-plant, hound's-tongue-plant, a rag [...] you fasten (it) with a tendon from a cow's carcass (or) the tendon of a dormouse; you put it around his neck.

r 88

[... ina SA] ⸢AB₂⸣.RI.RI.GA ina SA <PEŠ₂>.UR₃.RA GAG.GAGpi ina GU₂-šu₂ GARan1

r 99

[...] GIR₃.PAD.DU LU₂.U₁₈.LU tar-muš IGI-lim ŠAKIR AN.BAR NITA na₄ZALAG₂ na₄mu-ṣa ina KUŠ

(r 9) [...] a man's bone, lupin, imhur-līm-plant, henbane, male iron, zalāqu-stone, mūṣu-stone in a leather (bag).

r 1010

[...] tug₂NIG₂.DARA₂.ŠU.LAL₂ KAŠ SAG PIŠ₁₀.dID₂ na₄mu-ṣa ina KUŠ

(r 10) [...] a rag, first-class beer, sulphur, mūṣu-stone in a leather (bag).

r 1111

[...] x u₂IGI-lim u₂IGI.20 GIR₃.PAD.DU LU₂.U₁₈.LU ina KUŠ

(r 11) [...] imhur-līm-plant, imhur-ešrā-plant, a man's bone in a leather (bag).

r 1212

[...] NUMUN gišŠINIG SUḪUŠ u₂KUŠ₈!.LAGAB!(RA) an-ni ina I₃ EREN ḪI.ḪI ina KUŠ ina GU₂-šu₂ GAR ina I₃ EŠ

(r 12) [...] tamarisk seeds, colocynth root, you mix this up with cedar oil; you put (it) in a leather (bag) around his neck; you anoint (him) with oil.

r 1313

[...] ⸢giš⸣ŠINIG ina KAŠ ina I₃ ina KUŠ : * KI.MIN SUḪUŠ giššu-ši ina KAŠ ina I₃ ina KUŠ

(r 13) [...] of tamarisk, with beer, with oil in a leather (bag); : alternatively, liquorice root with beer, with oil in a leather (bag).

r 1414

[...] u₂AŠ.TAL₂.TAL₂ u₂el-kul-la u₂eli-kul-la

(r 14) [...] ardadillu-plant, elkullu-plant, elikulla-plant, [...] mūṣu-stone in a leather (bag).

r 1515

[...] ⸢na₄⸣mu-ṣa ina KUŠ

r 1616

[... u₂]⸢el⸣-kul-la

(r 16) [...] elkullu-plant

(rest of reverse missing)

1The Editor suggests to read AB₂.RI.RI.GA as šalquttu (following Labat) but the word is nowhere else attested. Another possibility would be to read it lītu and translate it as "dead cow" instead of "cow", as suggested by the authors of the CAD (CAD L: 217).
